meta.summary: ByteDance Seed officially launched Seed 2.0 and also previewed Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena, marking the clearest product artifact in the current cluster set. On the research side, new papers highlighted training and systems efficiency themes: "Pretraining large language models with MXFP4" targets FP4 stability for large-scale LLM training, while "PowerStep" proposes adaptive optimization without second-moment state to reduce memory overhead.

items.summary_short: We introduce a technique that enables Neural-ODEs to approximate arbitrary velocity fields with a priori planted fixed-points.

items.title: EquiMem: Calibrating Shared Memory in Multi-Agent Debate via Game-Theoretic Equilibrium
items.summary_short: Multi-agent debate (MAD) systems increasingly rely on shared memory to support long-horizon reasoning, but this convenience opens a critical vulnerability: a single corrupted entry can contaminate the downstream memory-augmented reasoning, and debate alone fails to filter such errors.
tags: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems

featured_topics.headline: ByteDance Seed Launches Seed 2.0 and Previews Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview
featured_topics.summary_short: ByteDance Seed officially launched Seed 2.0. The item describes it as a new model release from the company
featured_topics.why_it_matters: ByteDance is signaling continued foundation-model investment with a named model release, a concrete indicator for competitive model tracking and potential enterprise platform expansion.
key_takeaways: ByteDance Seed officially announced Seed 2.0 as a new model release through its own channels, making this the strongest confirmed product artifact in the dataset.
The company also released Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena, suggesting an external evaluation or early-access path ahead of broader deployment.
The launch was paired with ByteDance Seed's 2027 Foundation Model Campus Recruitment announcement, indicating parallel investment in model talent and long-term research capacity.

evidence.title: ByteDance Seed 2027 Foundation Model Campus Recruitment Open

featured_topics.headline: PowerStep Introduces Memory-Efficient Adaptive Optimization Without Second-Moment State
featured_topics.summary_short: This paper introduces PowerStep, a memory-efficient adaptive optimizer. It derives coordinate-wise adaptivity from transformed momentum without maintaining second-moment state
featured_topics.why_it_matters: Optimizer memory is a major bottleneck in large-model training; a credible adaptive method that removes second-moment state could improve batch size, hardware utilization, and training economics.
key_takeaways: PowerStep proposes coordinate-wise adaptivity derived from transformed momentum rather than maintaining the second-moment statistics used in optimizers such as Adam.
By eliminating second-moment state, the method targets lower optimizer-memory overhead, which is especially relevant for multi-billion-parameter training runs.
The paper frames the method through ℓ_p-norm steepest descent, positioning it as a theoretically grounded alternative rather than a purely heuristic optimizer tweak.

evidence.title: PowerStep: Memory-Efficient Adaptive Optimization via $\ell_p$-Norm Steepest Descent

featured_topics.headline: Paper 'Pretraining large language models with MXFP4' Targets FP4 LLM Training Stability
featured_topics.summary_short: This paper studies pretraining large language models with MXFP4. It focuses on the stability of FP4 pretraining for LLMs at large scale
featured_topics.why_it_matters: If validated, stable MXFP4 pretraining could materially lower training cost and memory requirements for frontier-scale LLMs, directly affecting infrastructure and model economics.
key_takeaways: The paper focuses on pretraining LLMs with MXFP4, a 4-bit floating-point approach aimed at pushing precision lower than standard FP8-era training pipelines.
Its central claim is FP4 pretraining stability, which is strategically important because instability has been a major blocker to using ultra-low precision throughout full training runs.
A workable MXFP4 recipe would matter most to hyperscalers and model labs optimizing GPU memory footprint, throughput, and total training cost for large-scale foundation models.

evidence.title: Pretraining large language models with MXFP4

topics.headline: Core-Halo Decomposition: Decentralizing Large-Scale Fixed-Point Problems
topics.summary_short: We study solving large-scale fixed-point equation \(x^\star=\bar F(x^\star)\) with decomposition. Standard strict decomposition assigns each agent a disjoint block and evaluates updates using only owned coordinates.
